questions: The `message_user`/`done` duplicate turn fix changes the turns array shape in the DB (removes the extra assistant turn). Does the frontend AgentChat component rely on `role === 'assistant'` turns to display agent messages from `message_user`? If so, we need to update it to also render `tool_result` turns where `tool_name === 'message_user'` — which file handles turn rendering?

Should `loadSessionStatus` and `persistSessionState` live in `loop.ts` (only consumer) or in a separate `db.ts` module? Keeping them in `loop.ts` is simpler; a separate `db.ts` is cleaner if other modules might need DB access later.

The `LOOP_LIMIT` (8) and `SOFT_TIMEOUT_MS` (50s) constants — should they stay as module-level constants in `loop.ts`, or move to a `config.ts` alongside the OpenRouter constants? Grouping all tunables in one file makes them easier to find but adds a file.
success_criteria: `index.ts` is under 80 lines — only HTTP handling and delegation
`deno check` (or equivalent) passes with no type errors across all new modules
The Llama fallback regex and parser block are completely removed
All tools dispatch through a single `TOOL_REGISTRY` lookup — no if/else chain for tool names
The `SupabaseAdmin` type is defined once in `types.ts` and used everywhere — no hand-typed inline interfaces
`createGenerationTask` returns error results instead of throwing on HTTP failures
`message_user` and `done` no longer create duplicate turns (tool_result + assistant with same content)
The function deploys successfully and passes a manual smoke test (send message, receive response, tool calls work)
The turns array format in `timeline_agent_sessions` is unchanged except for the removed duplicate assistant turns
No new features or behavior changes beyond the 4 specified fixes (Llama removal, duplicate turns, error handling, dispatch cleanup)
assumptions: The Llama fallback parser is fully dead code — minimax/m2.7 via OpenRouter always returns structured `tool_calls`, never `<function=name{args}</function>` text. Safe to delete.
The `logger.warn` call at `index.ts:695` (inside the Llama parser) confirms this code was speculative — `logger.warn` isn't even in the logger interface type. Another signal it's unused.

Removing the duplicate assistant turn for `message_user`/`done` is the correct fix. The tool_result turn already contains the full text, so the extra assistant turn is redundant data. If the frontend needs adjustment, that's a small follow-up in Step 14.

The `SupabaseAdmin` type should be a narrow structural type (matching the `.from()/.rpc()` shapes actually used), not the full `SupabaseClient` from `@supabase/supabase-js`. This avoids adding a new dependency and keeps Deno happy.
No tests exist for the agent loop itself (it's an edge function). Validation is via type-checking + manual smoke test + deployment.
The `withEdgeRequest` handler provides `supabaseAdmin` which is a Supabase client — we can type our `SupabaseAdmin` interface to match its actual shape without importing the SDK types.
